prompt: Create a photorealistic documentary-style street photograph of the entrance to a Chinese electronics factory in Shenzhen on an overcast day. Show a wide horizontal 4:3 view from across a concrete driveway, with a slightly dated early-2000s digital camera look, natural muted colors, soft gray sky, realistic perspective, and no cinematic grading. The main subject is a white ceramic-tile industrial building complex with pale blue horizontal trim, three stories at the front and a long two-to-three-story wing receding diagonally into the background. Place a covered entrance canopy at the front-left center, glass doors beneath it, many rectangular windows with greenish glass, and a few external air-conditioning units on the side wall. On the upper facade, include red Chinese wall text reading 质量第一 客户至上. Stretch a red safety banner across the driveway in front of the building with yellow Chinese text reading 安全来自长期警惕 事故源于瞬间麻痹. On the right, place a tall dark red-brown vertical gate pillar topped with a white globe lamp, with gold vertical Chinese company lettering reading 深圳市富恒昌电子有限公司. In front of the pillar and along the entrance, include a closed silver accordion-style metal retractable gate. Add exactly 6 visible people: three adult male workers walking out from the entrance in the foreground, one in a light blue short-sleeve shirt, one in pale work clothes, one in a white shirt and jeans; plus three smaller figures standing farther back near the doorway. Add exactly 5 visible vehicles or bikes: one white van at far left, one dark sedan with a blue license plate parked in front-left, one dark vehicle partly hidden inside the gate, one black bicycle leaning near the gate, and one motorbike/scooter parked at the far right near blue planters. Include a wooden utility pole near the left foreground with overhead power lines crossing the upper-left corner and small posters pasted on the pole. Add low shrubs in blue-tiled planters on the right, gray concrete pavement with cracks and seams, and surrounding tiled factory walls. Make the image convincingly real, like an ordinary factory entrance snapshot, with slight lens softness and mundane environmental detail. Avoid modern luxury architecture, dramatic lighting, crowds, logos other than the described Chinese signage, and any surreal or illustrative style.
